evals.prompt: My iOS app is draining battery. Can you audit it for energy issues?
evals.expected_output: Runs 7-phase iOS green audit: explores architecture, profiles energy, checks Creedengo iOS rules, reviews network/assets, measures SCI, produces report.
assertions: Identifies iOS-specific energy drains (location, networking, sensors)
Checks against Creedengo iOS rules (location precision, sensor polling, idle timer)
Recommends Xcode Instruments Energy Log or MetricKit for measurement
Produces a structured report with methodology section

evals.prompt: Review my Swift code for green coding patterns — I want to reduce the app's carbon footprint
evals.expected_output: Focuses on Phase 3 (code patterns) with Creedengo iOS rules plus general Swift energy patterns.
assertions: Scans Swift files for Creedengo iOS patterns
Checks for struct vs class preference, async/await usage, lazy loading
Does not require a full 7-phase audit for a code-only review
Cites Creedengo iOS as the source for code rules

evals.prompt: Measure the SCI for our app's main API call on iPhone
evals.expected_output: Guides through SCI measurement with iPhone-specific defaults (3W device power, 70000g embodied carbon).
assertions: Uses iPhone-appropriate default values for devicePowerW and embodiedTotalG
Suggests ContinuousClock or CFAbsoluteTimeGetCurrent for timing
Calculates SCI via MCP tool or manual formula
Includes methodology and sources in the report
